sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.68 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: For a finite group$G$and a permutation group$K$, let$b_G(K)$denote the number of conjugacy classes of regular subgroups of$K$isomorphic to~$G$. Does there exist a function~$f$such that$b_G(K) n^{f(r)}$for every abelian group$G$of order$n$and rank~$r$, and every group~$K$such that$K^{(2)}=K$? (See Archive 19.67 for the definition of~$K^{(2)}$.)

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
